LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANS LENDINGHOME FUNDING CORPORATION AND LENDINGHOME CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS' PAPERS ARE BEREFT OF INFORMATION THAT WOULD ALLOW A COURT TO DETERMINE THE NUMBER OF HOURS COUNSEL SPENT ON LITIGATION TASKS. EVEN DEFENDANTS' AUTHORITIES DO NOT PERMIT THIS. (SEE, E.G., WERSHBA V. APPLE COMPUTER, INC. (2001) 91 CAL.APP. 4TH 224, 255 ("ESTABLISHING THE NUMBER OF HOURS SPENT WORKING ON THE CASE"); STEINY & CO. V. CALIFORNIA ELECTRIC SUPPLY CO. (2000) 79 CAL.APP.4TH 285, 293 ("DETAILED EVIDENCE OF HOURS SPENT, TASKS CONCLUDED").) MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Apr-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF SANDRA WARD'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS

Appearances: Jamie D. Wells of McGuireWoods LLP (415.844.9944) for defendant and responding party; No other appearances.

This matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Granted as to the eighteenth affirmative defense and denied as to all other affirmative defenses. Neither law nor custom permits a defendant to reserve the right to assert additional affirmative defenses without seeking leave of court or the plaintiff's agreement and thus the eighteenth affirmative defense is invalid. This serves no useful purpose as to any of the other affirmative defenses. While some of the purported affirmative defenses are not truly affirmative defenses and others do not contain much or any factual allegations, all of the affirmative defenses except the eighteenth conform to the longstanding custom and practice of answers in Bay Area state courts. Almost assuredly very few of the affirmative defenses will be asserted in either motion practice or at trial and thus most of them are pled "out of abundance" caution in the event that facts are discovered that support them. This "abundance of caution" pleading of affirmative defenses, while perhaps not laudable, has been accepted for a long time. If Ms. Ward truly wants to learn the factual bases for the affirmative defenses and seek their disposition, she can check the appropriate boxes on the form interrogatories and requests for admissions and, if no facts are provided, can seek a stipulation that the affirmative defenses are not viable or, failing receipt of such a stipulation, file a motion for summary adjudication. Order signed. The Court also signed a stipulated protective order related to a discovery motion scheduled for this date. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Kelly DeYoung, CSR 6780 (kdeyoung@sftc.org) (302/HEK)
